Terms of Service

Professional service terms governing our commercial finance brokerage relationship. Compliant with Australian Consumer Law (ACL), ASIC Act 2001, and National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009.

Last Updated: 15 August 2025 Effective Date: 15 August 2025 Next Review: 15 August 2026

Service Agreement Overview

Australian Law Compliant

Our service terms comply with Australian Consumer Law, ASIC Act 2001, Corporations Act 2001, and National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009.

Professional Finance Brokerage

Licensed commercial finance brokerage services specialising in equipment finance, commercial property, and business growth solutions.

Fair Trading Standards

Transparent service delivery with clear obligations, fair commercial terms, and professional dispute resolution procedures.

ASIC Regulatory Compliance

All services provided under Australian Credit Licence obligations with professional indemnity insurance and regulatory oversight.

Quick Navigation

Service Agreement Service Description Client Obligations Fees & Payment Intellectual Property Liability & Disclaimers Termination Dispute Resolution Governing Law Contact Information

Service Agreement and Acceptance

About This Agreement

These Terms of Service ("Terms", "Agreement") constitute a legally binding agreement between Edgeview Finance Business Solutions Pty Ltd (ABN: [ABN Number], ACL: [ACL Number]) ("EVFBS", "we", "us", "our") and you ("Client", "you", "your") for the provision of commercial finance brokerage services.

Acceptance of Terms

By engaging our services, you acknowledge that you have:

  • Read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms
  • Authority to enter into this agreement on behalf of your business entity
  • Capacity to enter into legally binding agreements under Australian law
  • Received and understood our Credit Guide and Financial Services Guide

Service Commencement

Our service relationship commences when you submit a finance application or request professional consultation. These Terms apply to all services provided from that date forward.

Key Legislative Framework

Our services are governed by:

  • Australian Consumer Law (Competition and Consumer Act 2010)
  • Australian Securities and Investments Commission Act 2001 (Cth)
  • National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009 (Cth)
  • Corporations Act 2001 (Cth)
  • Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and Australian Privacy Principles
  • Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Act 2006 (Cth)

Updates to Terms

We may update these Terms to reflect changes in law, regulation, or business practices. Material changes will be communicated with 30 days' notice before taking effect. Continued use of our services constitutes acceptance of updated Terms.

Service Description and Scope

Commercial Finance Brokerage Services

We provide professional commercial finance brokerage services including:

Equipment Finance

  • Asset finance and equipment loans from $20,000 to $500,000+
  • Commercial vehicle and fleet financing
  • Construction and industrial equipment funding
  • Technology and specialised machinery finance
  • Chattel mortgage and finance lease structures

Commercial Property Finance

  • Business premises acquisition and investment property loans
  • Commercial property development funding
  • Owner-occupier and investment property refinancing
  • Construction and development finance facilities
  • Commercial mortgage and security arrangements

Working Capital Solutions

  • Business overdraft and line of credit facilities
  • Cash flow funding and debtor finance
  • Invoice factoring and discounting services
  • Trade finance and seasonal funding solutions
  • Working capital loans and business credit cards

Personal and Investment Lending

  • Home loans for business owners and self-employed
  • Investment property finance and equity release
  • Commercial to residential lending solutions
  • SMSF lending and structured finance
  • Personal loans secured against business assets

Professional Services Included

  • Strategic Finance Assessment: Comprehensive analysis of borrowing capacity and optimal finance structure
  • Lender Matching: Introduction to appropriate lenders from our professional network
  • Application Management: Preparation, submission, and management of finance applications
  • Negotiation Services: Rate, term, and condition negotiation on your behalf
  • Documentation Support: Assistance with loan documentation and settlement processes
  • Ongoing Relationship Management: Post-settlement support and future finance planning

Service Limitations and Exclusions

Our services do not include:

  • Legal advice (we recommend independent legal counsel)
  • Accounting or taxation advice (consult your accountant)
  • Investment advice or securities recommendations
  • Insurance brokerage (though we can provide referrals)
  • Business valuation or asset appraisal services
  • Guarantee or assurance of loan approval
  • Services to residential building industry (excluded by policy)

AI-Enhanced Service Delivery

Our services incorporate artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies to enhance:

  • Financial analysis and optimal structure identification
  • Lender matching based on risk profile and requirements
  • Application preparation and documentation efficiency
  • Market rate analysis and competitive positioning

All AI-enhanced services maintain human oversight and professional review to ensure accuracy and appropriateness.

Client Obligations and Responsibilities

Information Provision

You agree to provide:

  • Accurate Information: Complete, accurate, and current information about your financial position, business operations, and funding requirements
  • Required Documentation: All necessary documents including financial statements, tax returns, business registration details, and identification documents
  • Timely Updates: Prompt notification of any material changes to your financial circumstances or business operations
  • Responsive Communication: Timely responses to requests for additional information or clarification

Professional Conduct

You agree to:

  • Engage with lenders and third parties in a professional and courteous manner
  • Attend scheduled meetings and consultations as agreed
  • Comply with all lender requirements and conditions
  • Maintain confidentiality of commercially sensitive information
  • Provide reasonable notice for appointment changes or cancellations

Financial Obligations

You acknowledge responsibility for:

  • Payment of all agreed fees and charges as outlined in our Fee Disclosure Statement
  • Direct liaison with lenders regarding loan terms, conditions, and ongoing obligations
  • Independent verification of loan documents and terms before execution
  • Seeking independent legal and accounting advice where appropriate
  • Compliance with all loan conditions and ongoing obligations to lenders

Eligibility Requirements

To engage our services, you must:

  • Be at least 18 years of age and have legal capacity
  • Hold appropriate authority to bind your business entity
  • Operate an eligible business (excludes residential building industry)
  • Meet our client acceptance criteria and risk assessment requirements
  • Provide satisfactory identification and verification documents
  • Comply with Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing requirements

Prohibited Activities

You agree not to:

  • Provide false, misleading, or incomplete information
  • Use our services for illegal or fraudulent purposes
  • Circumvent our brokerage relationship by dealing directly with introduced lenders
  • Share confidential lender information with competitors
  • Engage in any activity that may damage our professional reputation

Important Notice: Independent Professional Advice

While we provide expert finance brokerage services, we strongly recommend seeking independent professional advice from:

  • Legal Counsel: For contract review, security arrangements, and legal implications
  • Accounting Advice: For taxation implications, business structure, and financial planning
  • Insurance Brokers: For appropriate business and asset protection

Fees and Payment Terms

Fee Structure

Our professional fees are disclosed in our Fee Disclosure Statement and may include:

Lender Commission

  • Upfront commission paid by successful lender (typically 0.5% - 1.5% of loan amount)
  • Trail commission for ongoing loans (typically 0.1% - 0.3% per annum)
  • No direct cost to client for standard brokerage services
  • Commission rates vary by lender and product type

Professional Service Fees

  • Application preparation and submission fee: $500 - $2,500 (complex applications)
  • Strategic finance consulting: $350 per hour (for specialised advice)
  • Document review and negotiation: $200 per hour
  • Expedited processing fee: $750 (rush applications)

Third-Party Costs

  • Valuation costs (passed through at cost)
  • Legal documentation fees (where arranged by us)
  • Credit report and verification costs
  • Government charges and duties (where applicable)

Payment Terms

  • Commission Payments: Received from lenders upon loan settlement
  • Professional Service Fees: Payable within 14 days of invoice date
  • Upfront Fees: May be required for complex applications before commencement
  • Third-Party Costs: Payable immediately upon invoice or as required by third party

Fee Refund Policy

Professional service fees may be refunded in the following circumstances:

  • Application withdrawal within 48 hours of engagement (70% refund)
  • Service failure to meet agreed timelines through our fault (pro-rata refund)
  • Material error in fee calculation (difference refunded)
  • Unsuccessful application due to material misrepresentation in our advice (full refund of professional fees)

Commission payments from lenders are not refundable as they compensate for completed professional services.

Late Payment

Late payment of professional service fees may result in:

  • Suspension of ongoing services until payment is received
  • Interest charges at Reserve Bank rate plus 5% per annum
  • Recovery costs including legal fees and collection expenses
  • Reporting to credit agencies for commercial customers

Transparent Fee Disclosure

We are committed to transparent fee disclosure in accordance with ASIC Regulatory Guide 273. All fees, commission arrangements, and potential conflicts of interest are disclosed in our:

  • Financial Services Guide (FSG)
  • Fee Disclosure Statement (provided with applications)
  • Statement of Advice (for complex arrangements)

You have the right to request details of all commission and fee arrangements before proceeding with any application.

Intellectual Property Rights

Our Intellectual Property

All intellectual property in our services, systems, and materials remains our exclusive property, including:

  • Technology Platforms: AI analysis tools, assessment algorithms, and proprietary software
  • Business Processes: Finance structuring methodologies, assessment frameworks, and service delivery processes
  • Marketing Materials: Website content, brochures, presentations, and promotional materials
  • Trade Marks: "Edgeview Finance", "EVFBS", logos, and associated branding
  • Databases: Lender networks, rate databases, and client management systems
  • Training Materials: Staff training content, procedure manuals, and process documentation

Client Use Rights

You are granted limited, non-exclusive rights to:

  • Use our services for your legitimate business finance requirements
  • Reference our involvement in your finance arrangements for business purposes
  • Retain copies of advice and documentation provided specifically to you
  • Use our contact information for ongoing relationship management

Restrictions on Use

You agree not to:

  • Copy, reproduce, or reverse-engineer our proprietary systems or processes
  • Use our intellectual property for competitive purposes
  • Modify, adapt, or create derivative works from our materials
  • Sublicense or transfer rights to third parties
  • Remove or alter intellectual property notices or attribution
  • Use our trade marks or branding without express written permission

Third-Party Intellectual Property

Our services may incorporate third-party intellectual property under appropriate licensing arrangements. We warrant that we have appropriate rights to provide these services but do not transfer ownership of third-party intellectual property to clients.

Intellectual Property Indemnity

We will defend and indemnify you against claims that our standard services infringe third-party intellectual property rights, provided you:

  • Promptly notify us of any infringement claims
  • Allow us to control the defence and settlement
  • Provide reasonable cooperation and assistance
  • Have used our services in accordance with these Terms

AI Technology and Data Rights

Our AI-enhanced services utilise proprietary algorithms and machine learning models. While you retain ownership of your submitted data, we may use anonymised and aggregated data to improve our services, subject to privacy protections and commercial confidentiality.

AI-generated insights and recommendations remain our intellectual property, though you receive a licence to use them for your business finance decisions.

Liability and Disclaimers

Service Disclaimers

While we provide professional services to high industry standards, we disclaim liability for:

  • Loan Approval: We cannot guarantee loan approval, specific terms, or settlement outcomes
  • Lender Decisions: Lender credit policies, assessment criteria, and approval decisions are beyond our control
  • Market Conditions: Interest rate changes, policy amendments, or market volatility affecting applications
  • Third-Party Services: Performance or quality of valuers, lawyers, accountants, or other professional services
  • Economic Factors: Economic downturns, regulatory changes, or industry-specific impacts

Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by Australian Consumer Law:

Professional Services

  • Our liability is limited to the professional indemnity insurance cover maintained ($2 million minimum)
  • Consequential, indirect, or economic loss is excluded except where prohibited by law
  • Total liability for any claim is capped at the fees paid for the relevant service
  • Claims must be notified within 12 months of the relevant service provision

Information and Advice

  • Information is provided in good faith based on current market conditions
  • Projections, estimates, and forecasts are indicative only
  • You should verify all information and seek independent advice
  • We are not liable for decisions based solely on our recommendations

Technology and Systems

  • AI and technology services are provided "as is" with reasonable accuracy expectations
  • System availability cannot be guaranteed due to maintenance, updates, or technical issues
  • Data loss or corruption liability is limited to data restoration where possible
  • Third-party technology integrations are subject to provider terms and limitations

Consumer Law Rights

Under Australian Consumer Law, you have rights that cannot be excluded, including rights to:

  • Services provided with due care and skill
  • Services fit for purpose and as described
  • Remedy for failure to meet consumer guarantees
  • Protection against misleading or deceptive conduct

Where consumer guarantees apply, our liability may not be limited except as permitted by the Competition and Consumer Act 2010.

Professional Indemnity Insurance

We maintain professional indemnity insurance covering:

  • Professional negligence and errors in advice
  • Breach of professional duty and fiduciary obligations
  • Intellectual property infringement claims
  • Privacy breaches and data protection failures
  • ASIC regulatory compliance and licensing breaches

Client Indemnity

You agree to indemnify us against claims arising from:

  • False, misleading, or incomplete information you provide
  • Your breach of these Terms or applicable laws
  • Your unauthorised use of our intellectual property
  • Third-party claims related to your business operations
  • Failure to comply with lender obligations or conditions

Important Notice: Risk and Responsibility

Commercial finance involves significant financial commitments and business risks. While we provide professional advice and brokerage services, ultimate responsibility for finance decisions remains with you.

We strongly recommend:

  • Independent legal review of all finance documentation
  • Professional accounting advice on tax and business implications
  • Comprehensive insurance coverage for financed assets
  • Conservative cash flow planning and risk management

Termination

Termination by Client

You may terminate our services at any time by:

  • Providing written notice to our office (email acceptable)
  • Requesting withdrawal of pending applications
  • Ceasing to engage our ongoing services

Termination does not affect obligations for completed services or fees already incurred.

Termination by EVFBS

We may terminate services immediately if:

  • You breach these Terms and fail to remedy within 7 days of notice
  • You provide false or misleading information materially affecting the service
  • Payment of fees is overdue by more than 14 days
  • Your conduct becomes unprofessional or damages our reputation
  • We determine that continuing services would breach our regulatory obligations
  • Your business becomes ineligible for our services (e.g., enters administration)

Effect of Termination

Upon termination:

  • Immediate Effect: No further services will be provided from the termination date
  • Outstanding Fees: All accrued fees and expenses become immediately payable
  • Pending Applications: We will notify lenders of withdrawal unless instructed otherwise
  • Documentation: We will provide copies of relevant documents and correspondence
  • Confidentiality: Mutual confidentiality obligations continue indefinitely
  • Commission Rights: We retain rights to commission for completed introductions

Post-Termination Obligations

Following termination, you agree to:

  • Complete payment of all outstanding fees and expenses
  • Return any confidential materials or intellectual property
  • Refrain from disparaging our reputation or services
  • Honour any ongoing lender relationships established through our introduction

Survival of Terms

The following provisions survive termination:

  • Fee payment obligations and commission entitlements
  • Confidentiality and intellectual property protections
  • Limitation of liability and indemnity provisions
  • Dispute resolution and governing law clauses
  • Data protection and privacy obligations

Commission and Trail Rights

Termination of services does not affect our entitlement to:

  • Upfront commission for loans settled through our introduction
  • Trail commission for existing loans during their term
  • Professional service fees for completed work
  • Ongoing relationship management of established loans

These rights are protected under standard industry practice and lender agreements.

Dispute Resolution

Dispute Resolution Process

We are committed to resolving disputes fairly and efficiently through our established complaints process.

1

Internal Resolution

Direct discussion with your service consultant or account manager to identify and resolve concerns promptly:

  • Immediate discussion of service concerns
  • Good faith negotiation and problem-solving
  • Escalation to senior management if required
  • Written confirmation of agreed resolutions
2

Formal Complaints Process

Written complaint to our Complaints Officer if internal resolution is unsuccessful:

  • Submit complaint in writing with supporting documentation
  • Acknowledgment within 7 days with reference number
  • Investigation and response within 30 days
  • Independent review and resolution recommendations
3

External Dispute Resolution

Referral to Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA) for independent resolution:

  • Free external dispute resolution service
  • Binding decisions on financial service disputes
  • Professional and impartial review process
  • Alternative to court proceedings

External Dispute Resolution Options

Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA)

Website: www.afca.org.au

Phone: 1800 931 678

Email: info@afca.org.au

Post: GPO Box 3, Melbourne VIC 3001

Independent resolution of financial service disputes with binding determinations up to $1 million.

Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC)

Website: www.asic.gov.au

Phone: 1300 300 630

Regulatory complaints about licensing breaches, professional conduct, or industry compliance issues.

Queensland Small Business Commissioner

Website: www.business.qld.gov.au

Phone: 1800 988 722

Mediation services for commercial disputes under $1 million between Queensland businesses.

Mediation and Arbitration

For disputes not resolved through AFCA, the parties agree to attempt resolution through:

  • Mediation: Facilitated negotiation through Resolution Institute certified mediators
  • Expert Determination: Industry expert assessment for technical finance disputes
  • Arbitration: Binding arbitration under Queensland Commercial Arbitration Act 2013

Legal Proceedings

Court proceedings may only be commenced after exhausting dispute resolution processes, except for:

  • Urgent injunctive relief or asset protection
  • Debt recovery for undisputed amounts
  • Intellectual property infringement claims
  • Regulatory compliance enforcement

Our Commitment to Fair Resolution

We maintain comprehensive complaints handling procedures and commit to:

  • Good faith participation in all dispute resolution processes
  • Fair and reasonable settlement offers where appropriate
  • Compliance with external determination decisions
  • Process improvements based on complaint feedback

Our goal is professional resolution that maintains ongoing business relationships where possible.

Governing Law and Jurisdiction

Applicable Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of Queensland and the Commonwealth of Australia, including:

  • Queensland: Contract law, commercial legislation, and state business regulations
  • Commonwealth: Consumer protection, financial services, corporations, and credit legislation
  • Australian Consumer Law: Consumer guarantees, unfair contract terms, and misleading conduct provisions
  • ASIC Regulations: Financial services licensing, conduct, and professional obligations

Jurisdiction

The parties submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Queensland and Federal courts sitting in Queensland for any legal proceedings related to these Terms.

Regulatory Framework

Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC)

  • Australian Credit Licence obligations and compliance
  • Professional conduct and client obligation standards
  • Financial services disclosure and advice requirements
  • Regulatory guidance and industry codes of practice

Australian Consumer Law (ACL)

  • Consumer guarantees for service quality and fitness for purpose
  • Unfair contract terms protection for small business contracts
  • Misleading and deceptive conduct prohibitions
  • Right to remedy for failure to meet consumer guarantees

National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009

  • Responsible lending obligations and assessment requirements
  • Credit assistance and brokerage service regulations
  • Disclosure obligations and client protection measures
  • Dispute resolution and enforcement mechanisms

Interpretation

In interpreting these Terms:

  • References to legislation include amendments and replacements
  • Singular includes plural and vice versa
  • Currency references are to Australian Dollars
  • Business days exclude weekends and Queensland public holidays
  • Time references are to Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST)
  • Headings are for convenience and do not affect interpretation

Severability

If any provision of these Terms is found to be unenforceable or invalid, the remaining provisions continue in full force and effect. Invalid provisions will be severed or modified to the minimum extent necessary to ensure enforceability.

Variation

These Terms may only be varied by written agreement signed by both parties, except for:

  • Updates required by law or regulation (with notice)
  • Administrative changes that do not affect rights or obligations
  • Fee schedule updates with 30 days' notice
  • Contact information or business structure changes

Australian Consumer Law Protection

These Terms are subject to Australian Consumer Law, which provides protections that cannot be excluded or limited. Where our services are acquired for business purposes and your business employs fewer than 20 people, you may also be entitled to small business protections under the ACL.

Nothing in these Terms excludes, restricts, or modifies any consumer guarantee, right, or remedy available under Australian Consumer Law where to do so would be prohibited.
